items.what: Generate mutant-killing tests for server/auth.js (cookie-security + session-expiry + core token/consent invariants) to raise the ~60% auth mutation score before any auth refactor is attempted.
items.why_now: Doctrine names this as a hard prerequisite: 'add mutant-killing tests BEFORE any auth refactor', and a prior mutation run left cookie-security/session-expiry mutants alive. auth.js is the single largest hotspot the machine cannot yet touch (NOGO, security surface); this evidence-generation is the ONLY thing that converts the auth-substrate-policy-split campaign from blocked to workable. Highest-leverage unblock in the portfolio.

evidence.citation: DOCTRINE:NAMED-TARGETS auth-mutation-killing-tests — 'auth mutation score ~60%; cookie-security and session-expiry mutants survived'

acceptance_criteria: Mutation run over server/auth.js reports the previously-surviving cookie-security and session-expiry mutants as KILLED
New tests pin token issuance/introspection and consent-grant invariants by name (not by reachability)
Auth mutation score materially above the ~60% baseline, with the delta reported

est_cost.basis: extrapolated from t1b-transport-shim-negotiation-evidence-0001 ($1.58) — a comparable characterization/evidence job on a seam surface

items.predicted_gain: Unblocks the biggest owned hotspot (auth.js) for future decomplection; kills known-surviving security mutants — real safety, not proxy.

items.what: Advance the runtime/index.js handleMsg (cc=194) decomplection: first land the two characterization-oracle packets (dispatch-guards, awaited-order), then the family/scope/checkpoint extracts they unblock.
items.why_now: handleMsg is the doctrine's explicitly named single highest-cc owned function (cc=194) and sits in the #2-attention file. The packet pool already decomposes it into evidence-first packets (hm-dispatch-guards-oracle-0001, hm-awaited-order-oracle-0002) that MUST precede the effectful/property-at-risk extracts (hm-reporting/record-scope/state-checkpoint/assistance) — matching doctrine's 'generate_evidence before preserve_refactor' ordering. Sequencing the oracles first is exactly how a staff engineer detonates a 194-cc hairball safely.

evidence.citation: DOCTRINE:NAMED-TARGETS runtime-handleMsg-decomplect — 'the highest-cc owned function; decomplect into named sub-handlers with explicit state'

acceptance_criteria: Both oracle packets land with direct-output-pinning tests (not integration-by-reachability) green as red-then-green
handleMsg cc measurably reduced via named sub-handlers with explicit state, behavior proven preserved against the runtime event-spine tests
Each extract passes the T1b/A2 gate: genuine hidden-state-made-explicit or real enclosing-cc reduction, not relocation (canon R5)

est_cost.basis: rt-index-handlemsg-a2-decomplect prior selection-ledger est $2.93 for the A2 loop; oracle packets extrapolate to ~$0.56 each

items.predicted_gain: Detonates the largest single cognitive-load concentration in the runtime; converts a 194-cc god-function into named, individually-testable handlers.

items.what: Judged A2 decomplection of server/index.js::startServer (cc=67, T2-async-order) and buildAsApp (cc=43, T2-property) — the two hairballs driving the top-attention file.
items.why_now: server/index.js is the highest-attention owned file in the repo (churn 325 × mass 218). Its cost is concentrated in startServer and buildAsApp, the #1 and #2 flagged functions by attention. The competing server-index-t0 packet only clears the marginal T0 tail (16.3%-by-mass) and was correctly NOT-chosen last agenda as a streetlight trap; the real leverage is judged decomplection of these two functions.
acceptance_criteria: startServer async-ordering seams named and made explicit without changing awaited order/count (T2-async invariant preserved)
buildAsApp property-shape branches decomplected with behavior proven against server integration tests + a different-model judge PASS
Enclosing cc genuinely reduced (not relocated); the T0-tail work explicitly left to the ratchet, not conflated with this
items.predicted_gain: Reduces cognitive load on the most-churned owned file's two hottest functions — highest attention-weighted leverage on the server side.

items.what: Build the NO-HIGH-COMPLEXITY-ANONYMOUS-CALLBACK lint/CI ratchet blocking new anonymous callbacks above a small cc threshold or capturing more than N nonlocal names.
items.why_now: Doctrine calls the upstream generation-ratchet 'possibly the single highest-leverage Memo-7 outcome' and 'likely more valuable than any T0 codemod' — the 84%-of-mass closure distribution is an AI-built-code smell that will regenerate without a prevention rule. Sensors confirm the live population: 189 callbacks, 104 T2, top callback cc=120. Cheap and durable; stops the distribution reforming faster than any downstream refactor can drain it.

evidence.citation: DOCTRINE §3 — 'Discovery ratchet (the callback/closure smell detector — likely more valuable than any T0 codemod)'

acceptance_criteria: A CI-runnable check (scripts/code-quality/check-callback-ratchet.mjs) fails on a new anonymous callback exceeding the cc/capture threshold
Existing callbacks are grandfathered (ratchet, not a mass-rewrite); baseline recorded so the count can only go down
The rule is documented as a generation policy agents must follow
items.predicted_gain: Freezes the complexity distribution from regenerating — compounding prevention that outlasts any single refactor.

items.what: B campaign to decide the /_ref/* vs /v1/owner/* near-duplicate control-plane conflation (run/schedule/revoke/reactivate exist under both prefixes with different auth guards; 70 of 100 ops are control-plane).
items.why_now: B-inventory §6.4 flags this as the clearest noun-conflation candidate and ratification-queue #2 as the 'largest conflation'. It is the dominant public-contract structural question and directly serves the doctrine's B 40-50% budget direction. Behavior-changing → proposal + owner ratification.

evidence.citation: B-INVENTORY §6.4 — 'The /_ref and /v1/owner planes are near-duplicate control surfaces … clearest noun-conflation candidate'
RATIFICATION-QUEUE #2 — '/_ref/* vs /v1/owner/* … (70 of 100 ops are control-plane)' status OPEN

A ratifiable proposal (collapse-to-one vs keep-both-with-documented-reason) recorded in the ratification queue

items.predicted_gain: Resolves the single largest public-surface conflation — 70% of the operation count sits in this plane.

items.what: Audit every skipped test (58 static skip markers across 51 files AND runtime-skipped subtests) — each skip gets keep-with-reason, fix, or delete.
items.why_now: Doctrine-named target. 58 static skips is a standing evidence-integrity hole: a skipped test is an unproven invariant masquerading as coverage, which directly feeds the streetlight failure mode (green-by-reachability). The audit is cheap discovery that converts silent gaps into explicit keep/fix/delete decisions and surfaces new evidence-generation leads.

evidence.citation: DOCTRINE:NAMED-TARGETS skipped-test-audit — 'each skip gets keep-with-reason, fix, or delete … audit both' (static + runtime)

acceptance_criteria: Every static skip marker (58) triaged to keep-with-explicit-reason / fix / delete
Runtime '# skipped' subtests enumerated separately (the static count != runtime count) and triaged
Fix/delete leads emitted as follow-on packets; kept skips carry a written justification

est_cost.basis: discovery/triage pass; extrapolated from cheap evidence jobs (~$0.5) in cost-actuals

items.predicted_gain: Closes an evidence-integrity blind spot; each resolved skip either restores a real invariant or removes dead ceremony.

items.what: T1a clean hoist of server/search-semantic.js semanticIndexBackfillForManifest (cc=120, non-capturing, free-vars=∅) to a named pure top-level function.
items.why_now: This is the single largest callback smell in the repo (cc=120) and the callback detector explicitly classifies it as the cheap case: non-capturing (free-vars=∅) → hoist to a named pure top-level fn. T1a is the doctrine's genuinely-cheap seam lane; the maps to server-search-semantic-t1a-230e24ed in the pool. Rare high-mass + low-risk combination.

evidence.citation: SENSOR:CALLBACK-SMELLS — 'semanticIndexBackfillForManifest · other · cc=120 · excess=115 · 0(0) · T1a · non-capturing (free-vars=∅) → hoist to a named pure top-level fn'

Substrate/policy seam proposed behind a small interface as a B proposal
No autonomous refactor of auth.js at any point (NOGO); all changes human-ratified

not_chosen.reason: auth.js is NOGO in HOTSPOTS (score=68591), a security surface; doctrine forbids autonomous auth work and requires auth-mutation-killing-tests first. Routed to the b-auth-substrate-policy-split campaign, gated behind the mutation tests (item #1). Age 1 — not yet at run-floor.
Streetlight trap: the T0 tail is marginal mass (16.3% by mass across the repo) while the file's real cost is startServer/buildAsApp, chosen as A2 (item #3). Age 1.
Same streetlight trap — clears the low-mass T0 tail while handleMsg (cc=194) is the real target, addressed by the hm-* campaign (item #2). Age 1.

records.js mass lives in queryRecords (cc=99, T2-property) and the cross-binding aggregators (cc=73 each, T2-async) — T2-in-disguise, not a cheap T1a exact-move. Route to judged A2 later; not this cycle's budget. Age 1.

The prior runtime-scheduler-t0 attempt REVERTED at $2.93 with a REJECT judge (COST-ACTUALS); lower attention than the chosen A2 targets. Defer until the handleMsg/startServer A2 lane is proven. Age 1.
owner_ratify gate + a delete against a claims ledger I did not author; guidance is to surface not proceed. Raised in human_decisions_needed. Age 1.
db.js mass is in the .transaction callbacks (cc=52/39, T2-property, DDL markers CREATE/DROP TABLE) — T2-in-disguise requiring full judged proof, not a T0 transform. Age 1.
DONE — B-INVENTORY §6.1 shows RESOLVED 2026-07-01 (commit ce8dfc58b, 10 by-name tests at packages/mcp-server/test/content-ladder-contract.test.js). Dropped from this agenda.

BLOCKED: packet pool + cost-actuals show a red baseline — rung 'direct-test' failed BEFORE any change (exit 1, expected 0). Cannot land on a red baseline; needs the baseline test fixed first (deterministic-floor territory, not an agenda choice).

deltas_from_prior: DROPPED prior #1 b-content-ladder-direct-test-coverage — B-INVENTORY §6.1 now marks it RESOLVED (commit ce8dfc58b, 10 by-name tests); no longer a gap.

PROMOTED auth-mutation-killing-tests to #1 — it was only implicit in the prior agenda (via the auth NOT-chosen note); doctrine makes it the hard gate that unblocks the entire auth campaign, so it leads.
RE-EXPRESSED the handleMsg A2 target (#2) as an oracle-first packet sequence (hm-*-oracle then hm-*-extract) rather than a single monolithic A2 item, matching the pool's evidence-first decomposition.
MERGED startServer and buildAsApp into one server/index.js A2 item (#3) — prior agenda listed only startServer; buildAsApp (cc=43) is the same file's #2 hairball and belongs in the same decomplect.
ADDED b-storage-backend-contract-unification as an explicit B campaign — a doctrine-named target absent from the prior agenda's ranked items.
ADDED skipped-test-audit (#7) — doctrine-named target not previously ranked; 58 static skips is a standing evidence-integrity gap.

CARRIED the cheap proven lanes (df-evidence-package-schema-merge-oracle, t1a-semantic-backfill-hoist, t1b-streaming-input-telemetry-event, rt-verdict-stream-rollups-oracle, t1b-transport-shim-negotiation) with an explicit gate on transport after its prior REVERT.
human_decisions_needed: Approve the auth campaign gating: confirm that auth-mutation-killing-tests must land and kill the cookie-security/session-expiry mutants BEFORE any auth-substrate-policy-split refactor is proposed (doctrine gate — needs owner sign-off to proceed).
/_ref/* vs /v1/owner/* control planes (ratification #2): are these two genuine audiences, or one concept with two skins that should collapse? (behavior-changing, 70/100 ops).
storage-backend-contract-unification: ratify unifying postgres-storage.js and the sqlite backend behind a single storage interface — this is behavior/architecture-changing and cannot be auto-landed.
repo-tmp-claims-exhaust-0005: authorize (or decline) the owner_ratify delete of tmp-claims.jsonl — I will not delete a claims ledger I did not author without explicit owner ratification.

ESCALATION — no doctrine-named target was demoted below its worth this cycle; all six are ranked or campaign-tracked. Confirm the sequencing of auth work behind its mutation-test gate is acceptable rather than running auth as a B-inventory pass sooner.
